assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: The report summary table contains: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive test changes detected (see detailed assessment below) |'. The detailed section heading also states: '**Classification: MIXED** -- both additive and reductive signals are present in the test changes.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: The structural assessment table shows '-1 (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` removed)' under Reductive. The Reductive Signals section details: '1. **Removed test function: `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`** ... **Classification:** REDUCTIVE -- a test function covering qualifier-specific behavior was removed.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: The reductive findings section item 2 details: '**Relaxed assertion in `test_recommend_purls_basic`** ... Base branch: assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"); ... PR branch: assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"); ... **Classification:** Potentially REDUCTIVE -- the assertion no longer validates the full PURL string including qualifiers.' The structural table also shows '-1 (basic test relaxed from checking full PURL with qualifiers to checking versioned PURL without qualifiers)' under Assertion specificity.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural assessment includes: '**File: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs` (new)** This is an entirely new file with 3 test functions: - `test_simplified_purl_no_version` ... - `test_simplified_purl_mixed_types` ... - `test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved` ... Structural tally for `purl_simplify.rs`: +3 test functions, +11 assertions (all additive, new file)'. The additive signals section also states: '**New test file: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`** (3 new test functions)'.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: The structural assessment table shows '+1 (`test_recommend_purls_dedup` added)' under Additive. The Additive Signals section states: '1. **New test function: `test_recommend_purls_dedup`** (in `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`) - Tests deduplication of entries that become identical after qualifier removal - Seeds two PURLs with the same version but different qualifiers, asserts only one entry is returned - Covers new behavior introduced by this PR'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The output directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). These are verification report outputs, not code file modifications. The report reads and analyzes diffs and test content but does not indicate any code files were modified.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents a summary table with findings. There is no indication of any merge action being taken. The report presents acceptance criteria details, test change classification analysis, and structural/semantic assessments for human review.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: The summary table includes '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive test changes detected (see detailed assessment below) |'. This is followed by a detailed section '### Test Change Classification: MIXED' containing a Structural Assessment (with per-file tables and tallies), a Reductive Signals (detailed) section with 2 items, an Additive Signals (detailed) section with 2 items, and a Semantic Assessment section explaining the rationale.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural assessment explicitly states: 'Comparison of base-branch version (from `test-base-purl-recommend.md`) with PR version (from diff)'. The reductive signals reference base branch line numbers: 'Base branch (lines 30-48 of `test-base-purl-recommend.md`)' and 'Base branch (line 24-27 of `test-base-purl-recommend.md`)'. The analysis compares actual test code content (function names, assertion statements, PURL strings) between base and PR branches, identifying specific additions/removals/relaxations. While the semantic assessment does mention 'feature requirements' to contextualize intent, the structural classification itself is based on file content comparison.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The Test Quality row in the summary table states: 'All test functions have doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected; Eval Quality: N/A'. The Review Feedback row shows 'N/A | No review comments on the PR', confirming no reviews exist. Since Eval Quality is N/A, it does not affect the Test Quality combination.
